description: Integrate Stripe billing with subscription tiers, webhooks, and usage metering
system_context: SaaS app needs billing. Tech stack: Next.js 14, TypeScript, tRPC, Prisma, PostgreSQL. Integrating Stripe with 3 tiers (Free, Pro $29/mo, Team $99/mo/seat), webhook handling, usage metering for API calls, customer portal. 45 existing tests. Stripe test keys already in .env.
turns: Installed dependencies: npm install stripe @stripe/stripe-js. Stripe SDK v14.x. Created src/server/stripe.ts with new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, { apiVersion: '2024-04-10' }). Typed singleton with env validation at startup.

Created Stripe products in dashboard. Products: Pro (price_1QXyz... $29/mo recurring), Team (price_1QXyw... $99/mo/seat recurring with usage-based component for API calls over quota). Stored price IDs in .env as STRIPE_PRICE_PRO and STRIPE_PRICE_TEAM.

Updated Prisma schema. Added to User: stripeCustomerId (String? unique), stripeSubscriptionId (String? unique), subscriptionStatus (enum: trial, active, past_due, canceled, none), subscriptionTier (enum: free, pro, team). Added model Subscription with full Stripe mirror (status, current_period_end, cancel_at_period_end). Ran migration.

Built checkout flow. src/server/api/billing.ts — tRPC router. Mutation createCheckoutSession(priceId): creates stripe.checkout.sessions with mode=subscription, client_reference_id=userId, success_url, cancel_url, allow_promotion_codes. Returns session URL. 78 lines. 6 tests (with Stripe mocks).
Built customer portal access. Mutation createPortalSession(): creates stripe.billingPortal.sessions for user's stripeCustomerId. Returns portal URL. 22 lines. 3 tests.

Webhook endpoint at src/pages/api/webhooks/stripe.ts. Uses raw body (disabled Next.js body parser). Validates signature with stripe.webhooks.constructEvent and STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET. Handles 8 event types: checkout.session.completed, customer.subscription.updated, customer.subscription.deleted, invoice.payment_succeeded, invoice.payment_failed, customer.subscription.trial_will_end, customer.updated, customer.deleted. 187 lines.

Wrote webhook tests. src/pages/api/webhooks/__tests__/stripe.test.ts. 14 tests. Each test constructs a real Stripe event payload, signs it with stripe.webhooks.generateTestHeaderString, posts to the endpoint, verifies DB state changes. All 14 pass.

Idempotency. Added webhook_events table (Prisma). Stores stripe event ID as unique key. Before processing, INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. If conflict, skip processing (already handled). 3 tests verify idempotency.

Subscription sync. Webhook handler updates User.subscriptionStatus, User.subscriptionTier, User.stripeSubscriptionId on every event. Tested the full lifecycle: subscribe → paid → update → cancel. Local testing with stripe trigger commands.

Usage metering for API calls. Team tier has 100k API calls included, $0.001 per call over. Created src/server/usage.ts — tracks api_calls in Redis by user_id, period. Nightly cron (Upstash QStash) rolls up daily totals to Postgres usage_records table. 112 lines.

On invoice.created webhook, push usage to Stripe: stripe.subscriptionItems.createUsageRecord with quantity=excess_calls. 34 lines added to webhook handler. 4 tests covering under-quota, at-quota, over-quota scenarios.

Built src/middleware/subscription-gate.ts. tRPC middleware checks user.subscriptionTier against route.requiredTier. If insufficient, throws TRPCError UPGRADE_REQUIRED with upgradeUrl. Applied to all Pro/Team-only procedures. 56 lines. 9 tests.

Built pricing page at src/pages/pricing.tsx. Three cards (Free, Pro, Team). For each tier: bullet list of features, checkout button (disabled if already on that tier). Checkout button calls createCheckoutSession and redirects. 167 lines TSX. Mobile-responsive.
Built billing page at src/pages/settings/billing.tsx. Shows current plan, next billing date, usage meter (X / 100k API calls), button to open Stripe customer portal. 134 lines. 5 tests.
Edge case: trial_will_end webhook (3 days before trial ends). Sends email via Resend with upgrade link. Stored in email_sent table to prevent duplicate sends. 4 tests.

Edge case: past_due webhook. Marks user subscriptionStatus=past_due but doesn't lock features (grace period). After 14 days past_due → Stripe cancels → subscription.deleted webhook → downgrade to free tier. Tested with stripe clock advance.

Webhook retry handling. Stripe retries failed webhooks with exponential backoff. If our handler fails (DB down etc), we return 500 and Stripe retries. On successful retry, idempotency check prevents double-processing. Tested manually.

Local webhook testing. Set up stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks/stripe. stripe trigger customer.subscription.created, stripe trigger invoice.payment_failed, etc. All events processed correctly.

End-to-end test. Created test user, went through checkout flow, became Pro, used a Pro-only feature (worked), opened customer portal, canceled, waited for period end via stripe clock, subscription ended, Pro features locked out. Full flow works.
Final test suite: 98/98 passing (45 existing + 53 new billing tests). All Stripe flows verified. Ready for staging deploy.
